capability_objective: I’m reconciling two records for a handoff. Search separately for “Microeconomics evening seminar” in Downtown Campus and “Modern history survey” in North Campus, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete course records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Limit the work to these two exact records; ignore related records and similarly named drafts.
prompt:
Reconcile the two course records in this sandbox and create course-handoff.md. Use the genuine local ./coursectl executable through Pi's Bash tool as the sole interface to course data. You may inspect its built-in help before the first course-data operation. Do not inspect or modify the executable, its backing or runtime data, its audit evidence, or protected verification files, and do not manufacture execution evidence. Do not write a wrapper or orchestration script.

Search separately for the exact course name “Microeconomics evening seminar” at “Downtown Campus” and the exact course name “Modern history survey” at “North Campus”. These searches are independent: start them as exactly two sibling Bash calls in one assistant action, with one direct course operation in each call. Do not combine them in one shell command or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Continue only if each search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either branch is missing, ambiguous, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval, do not create the report, and identify the unresolved branch without guessing.

After both searches resolve, retrieve the two complete records using only their respective returned IDs. Start both retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash calls in the next assistant action that accesses course data, again with one direct course operation in each call. Do not start either retrieval until both searches have finished. Base the status and date comparison only on fields in the complete records, never on search summaries.

This is a read-only, two-record handoff. Invoke no course operation other than the two searches and two retrievals. Do not update, cancel, or notify. Do not touch drafts, related courses, similarly named courses, records at other campuses, or any other record. Leave all course and notification state unchanged and leave no scratch artifacts.

When both complete records are available, write course-handoff.md as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly four newline-terminated plain-text lines with no heading, bullets, or blank lines. Put Microeconomics evening seminar first and Modern history survey second. Format the first two lines as `<name> | <stable ID> | <campus> | <status> | <date>`, preserving returned values exactly. Format the third line as `Comparison | status: <same|different> (<first status> vs <second status>) | date: <same|different> (<first date> vs <second date>)`. Make the final line exactly `No changes made.` Run python3 -B .protected/verify.py after writing the report; this command is the only permitted access to .protected. Return exactly the report content as the final response.
